The burden of proof is on the trademark owner to show use or proper reasons for non-use. The UK and EU case law on genuine use (e.g., La Mer v. La Mer, C-171/06) is persuasive in HK. The revocation proceedings are heard by the Registrar or the court. See the general principles of trademark revocation and non-use applied in HK.
notes: 三法域高度对应：CN法下（商标法第49条）连续三年不使用可撤销；US法下（15 U.S.C. §1058, §1127）3 years non-use is prima facie abandonment；HK法下（Cap 559 §52）3 years non-use without genuine use or proper reasons for revocation。核心法理一致：non-use for 3 consecutive years is grounds for cancellation/revocation，registrant may defend by showing use or proper reasons。positive_control=True。

fact_summary: 企业在商品上使用与他人有一定影响的商品名称近似的标识，导致消费者混淆/已验证:trade name confusion mapped
cn_claim: 依据《反不正当竞争法》第6条第1项，经营者不得擅自使用与他人有一定影响的商品名称、包装、装潢等相同或近似的标识，引人误认为是他人商品或与他人存在特定联系。该保护不以注册为前提，但以'有一定影响'为要件。
us_claim: Under US law, the Lanham Act, 15 U.S.C. § 1125(a), protects unregistered trade names and trade dress against false designation of origin and unfair competition. The plaintiff must prove that the mark or trade dress has acquired secondary meaning (distinctiveness through use) and that the defendant's use is likely to cause confusion. Under the Supreme Court in Two Pesos v. Taco Cabana, 505 U.S. 763 (1992), trade dress may be inherently distinctive. Under Wal-Mart v. Samara, 529 U.S. 205 (2000), product design trade dress is never inherently distinctive and must prove secondary meaning. Under the state law of unfair competition, the plaintiff may also have a claim under the common law. See the general principles of trademark and unfair competition law applied in US law.
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong law, the common law tort of passing off protects unregistered trade names, trade marks, and get-up against misrepresentation. The plaintiff must prove goodwill (reputation), misrepresentation (likely to deceive the public), and damage (actual or likely loss of business). The tort of passing off does not require registration and protects against unfair competition. See Reckitt & Colman v. Borden [1990] 1 WLR 491 (UK, applied in HK) for the classic trinity of passing off. The Trade Marks Ordinance (Cap 559) also protects registered trade marks, but passing off provides a parallel remedy for unregistered marks. The Trade Descriptions Ordinance (Cap 362) provides criminal penalties for false trade descriptions. The common law of passing off is the primary remedy for unregistered trade name confusion in HK. See the general principles of passing off and unfair competition applied in HK.
notes: 三法域高度对应：CN法下（反不正当竞争法第6条）有一定影响的商品名称受保护；US法下（Lanham Act §1125(a)）unregistered marks protected by secondary meaning + likelihood of confusion；HK法下（common law passing off, Reckitt & Colman v. Borden）goodwill + misrepresentation + damage。核心法理一致：unregistered trade names may be protected against confusion/unfair competition if they have acquired reputation/goodwill/secondary meaning。positive_control=True。

fact_summary: 出版社未经作者同意将其作品改编为电影剧本，作者主张侵犯改编权/已验证:derivative work adaptation mapped
cn_claim: 依据《著作权法》第10条第1款第14项，改编权即改变作品，创作出具有独创性的新作品的权利。未经著作权人许可，改编其作品的，构成侵权，应承担停止侵害、赔偿损失等民事责任。
us_claim: Under US copyright law, 17 U.S.C. § 106(2), the copyright owner has the exclusive right to prepare derivative works based on the copyrighted work. A derivative work is a work based upon one or more preexisting works, such as a translation, musical arrangement, dramatization, fictionalization, motion picture version, sound recording, art reproduction, abridgment, condensation, or any other form in which a work may be recast, transformed, or adapted. Under 17 U.S.C. § 501, the unauthorized preparation of a derivative work is infringement. Under the fair use doctrine, 17 U.S.C. § 107, the unauthorized adaptation may be permissible if it is transformative and does not substitute for the original work. See Campbell v. Acuff-Rose Music, 510 U.S. 569 (1994).
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong copyright law, the Copyright Ordinance (Cap 528) protects the right to make adaptations of a work. Under Section 23, an adaptation of a literary work includes a translation, a version in which the story or action is conveyed by pictures, or a dramatization. Under Section 22, copyright infringement occurs when a person does any of the acts restricted by copyright without a license. The preparation of a derivative work without the copyright owner's permission is infringement. The fair dealing exceptions (Section 39) may apply if the adaptation is for research, private study, criticism, review, or reporting current events. The Copyright Ordinance does not have a general fair use provision. The copyright owner may seek remedies including injunctions, damages, and delivery up. See the general principles of copyright and derivative works applied in HK.
notes: 三法域高度对应：CN法下（著作权法第10条）改编权为专有权利；US法下（17 U.S.C. §106(2)）exclusive right to prepare derivative works；HK法下（Cap 528 §23, §22）right to make adaptations + infringement for unauthorized adaptation。核心法理一致：adaptation/derivative work right is exclusive to the copyright owner，unauthorized adaptation is infringement unless fair use/fair dealing applies。positive_control=True。

fact_summary: 软件公司主张其软件源代码被前员工复制到新雇主的产品中，要求禁止销售侵权产品/已验证:software copyright infringement mapped
cn_claim: 依据《著作权法》第3条、第53条，计算机软件属于著作权法保护的作品。未经著作权人许可，复制、发行其软件的，构成侵权。软件著作权人可请求停止侵害、赔偿损失。对于侵权复制品，法院可判令销毁。
us_claim: Under US copyright law, 17 U.S.C. § 101 defines a computer program as a set of statements or instructions to be used directly or indirectly in a computer in order to bring about a certain result. Under 17 U.S.C. § 106, the copyright owner has the exclusive rights to reproduce, distribute, and create derivative works. Under 17 U.S.C. § 501, the unauthorized copying of a computer program is infringement. Under the landmark case Whelan v. Jaslow, 797 F.2d 1222 (3d Cir. 1986), the structure, sequence, and organization (SSO) of a program may be protected. However, under Computer Associates v. Altai, 982 F.2d 693 (2d Cir. 1992), the abstraction-filtration-comparison test is used to determine whether non-literal elements of a program are protected. Under the DMCA, 17 U.S.C. § 1201, circumvention of technological protection measures is prohibited. Under 17 U.S.C. § 502, the court may grant injunctions to prevent infringement.
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong copyright law, the Copyright Ordinance (Cap 528) protects computer programs as literary works. Under Section 2, a literary work includes a computer program. Under Section 22, copyright infringement occurs when a person copies the whole or a substantial part of a work without a license. Under Section 18, copying includes reproducing the work in any material form, including storing the work in electronic form. Under Section 118, the copyright owner may seek remedies including injunctions, damages, and delivery up. The courts have applied the general principles of copyright law to software, including the distinction between idea and expression. See the general principles of software copyright applied in HK. The Copyright Ordinance does not have a specific provision for software infringement but applies the general provisions for literary works. The Copyright (Amendment) Ordinance 2020 introduced new provisions for technology-neutral protection.
notes: 三法域高度对应：CN法下（著作权法第3条、第53条）计算机软件为作品，未经许可复制构成侵权；US法下（17 U.S.C. §101, §106, §501）computer program is literary work，unauthorized copying is infringement，SSO/Altai test for non-literal elements；HK法下（Cap 528 §2, §22, §18）computer program is literary work，copying includes electronic storage，substantial part test applies。核心法理一致：computer software is protected as a literary work，unauthorized copying is infringement，courts distinguish idea from expression。positive_control=True。

fact_summary: 企业在商品上使用与他人注册商标相同的标识，但主张该标识为描述性使用/已验证:descriptive use defense partial
cn_claim: 依据《商标法》第59条，注册商标中含有的本商品的通用名称、图形、型号，或直接表示商品的质量、主要原料、功能、用途、重量、数量及其他特点，或含有的地名，注册商标专用权人无权禁止他人正当使用。描述性使用须为非商标性使用，且不导致混淆。
us_claim: Under US trademark law, the Lanham Act, 15 U.S.C. § 1115(b)(4), provides a fair use defense for descriptive use of a term otherwise than as a mark. The use must be in good faith and only to describe the goods or services. Under the doctrine of nominative fair use, the defendant may use the plaintiff's mark to refer to the plaintiff's product if there is no other way to identify the product and the use does not suggest sponsorship. See New Kids on the Block v. News America Publishing, 971 F.2d 302 (9th Cir. 1992). Under KP Permanent Make-Up v. Lasting Impression, 543 U.S. 111 (2004), the fair use defense is available even if there is likelihood of confusion. The defendant bears the burden of proving fair use.
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong trademark law, the Trade Marks Ordinance (Cap 559) does not have a specific fair use defense for descriptive use. However, under Section 15, a trademark may not be registered if it is descriptive or devoid of distinctive character, unless it has acquired distinctiveness through use. Under Section 46, the use of a registered mark may not be infringed if it is in accordance with honest practices. The common law defense of honest concurrent use may apply. The UK and EU case law on descriptive use (e.g., Arsenal v. Reed, C-206/01) is persuasive in HK. The defense of descriptive use is less developed in HK than in the US. The Trade Marks Ordinance does not have a specific provision for fair use or nominative fair use. The general principles of honest use and the need to avoid confusion apply. See the general principles of trademark law and the defenses applied in HK.
notes: 三法域部分对应：CN法下（商标法第59条）正当使用描述性标识不侵权；US法下（15 U.S.C. §1115(b)(4), New Kids on the Block, KP Permanent Make-Up）descriptive fair use + nominative fair use defenses well-developed；HK法下（Cap 559 §15, §46）descriptive use limited，honest practices defense less developed。核心差异：US fair use defense most developed and protective，CN has statutory fair use provision，HK defense less developed relying on general principles。hard_case=True。

fact_summary: 艺术家主张其街头涂鸦作品被商业广告使用，要求停止使用并赔偿/已验证:street art copyright partial
cn_claim: 依据《著作权法》第3条、第10条，美术作品受著作权法保护，包括绘画、雕塑、书法等。街头涂鸦若具有独创性，可作为美术作品受保护。但涂鸦若在未经授权的墙面上创作，可能涉及侵权或违法行为，影响其版权保护。
us_claim: Under US copyright law, 17 U.S.C. § 102(a)(5), pictorial, graphic, and sculptural works are protected. Under the Supreme Court in Feist, originality is the touchstone of copyright. Under the Visual Artists Rights Act (VARA), 17 U.S.C. § 106A, the author of a work of visual art has the right to prevent the destruction of a work of recognized stature. Under the Fifth Circuit in Villa v. Pearson Education, 2019 WL 1138335 (5th Cir. 2019), graffiti may be protected by copyright if it is original. However, under the illegality doctrine, some courts have held that a work created in violation of the law may not be protected by copyright. See the debate on the 'illegality doctrine' in US copyright law. Under the doctrine of fair use, the commercial use of graffiti in advertising may be challenged. Under the Lanham Act, the use of the graffiti may also give rise to a claim of false endorsement.
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong copyright law, the Copyright Ordinance (Cap 528) protects artistic works, including paintings and drawings. Under Section 2, an artistic work includes a painting, drawing, diagram, map, chart, plan, engraving, etching, lithograph, woodcut, or similar work. Under Section 4, the work must be original. Under the common law, the copyright in a work created in breach of the law (e.g., trespass, vandalism) may still be protected, but the copyright owner may be unable to enforce the copyright if they have unclean hands. Under the general principles of equity, the court may refuse to grant an injunction or damages to a plaintiff who has acted in bad faith. However, there is no specific HK case law on the copyrightability of graffiti. The UK and EU case law on illegal works and copyright is persuasive. The Copyright Ordinance does not have a specific provision for illegal works. The general principles of copyright and equity would apply. See the general principles of copyright law applied in HK.
notes: 三法域部分对应：CN法下（著作权法第3条）涂鸦作品若独创性可保护，但违法创作可能影响保护；US法下（17 U.S.C. §102, VARA, Feist）graffiti may be protected but illegality doctrine may bar enforcement；HK法下（Cap 528 §2, §4）artistic works protected but unclean hands doctrine may limit enforcement。核心差异：三法域均可能保护原创涂鸦，但违法创作对版权保护的影响不同——US有illegality doctrine debate，HK有unclean hands，CN司法实践尚不明确。hard_case=True。

fact_summary: 企业主张其专利被他人通过等价替换方式侵犯，要求停止侵权/已验证:doctrine of equivalents partial
cn_claim: 依据《专利法》第64条，发明或实用新型专利权的保护范围以其权利要求的内容为准，说明书及附图可以用于解释权利要求的内容。中国专利法虽未明文规定'等同原则'，但司法实践中已广泛采用'等同侵权'判断标准，即被控侵权技术方案中的某个技术特征与权利要求中的对应技术特征以基本相同的手段，实现基本相同的功能，达到基本相同的效果，且本领域普通技术人员无需创造性劳动即可联想到的，构成等同侵权。
us_claim: Under US patent law, 35 U.S.C. § 271, patent infringement includes literal infringement and infringement under the doctrine of equivalents. Under the doctrine of equivalents, a product or process that does not literally infringe a patent claim may still be found to infringe if it performs substantially the same function in substantially the same way to obtain the same result. Under the Supreme Court in Warner-Jenkinson v. Hilton Davis, 520 U.S. 17 (1997), the doctrine of equivalents is available unless the patentee has surrendered the equivalent subject matter during prosecution (prosecution history estoppel). Under Festo v. Shoketsu, 535 U.S. 722 (2002), prosecution history estoppel may bar the application of the doctrine of equivalents. Under the all-limits rule, every element of the claim must be present, either literally or by an equivalent. Under the doctrine of vitiation, if the equivalent would read on the claim in a way that vitiates the claim limitation, the doctrine does not apply. The doctrine of equivalents is a judicially created doctrine, not a statutory provision.
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong patent law, the Patents Ordinance (Cap 514) does not have a specific provision for the doctrine of equivalents. Under the common law and UK precedents, the scope of patent protection is determined by the claims, interpreted in light of the specification and the knowledge of the skilled person at the priority date. Under the UK and EPO approach (which HK follows), the doctrine of equivalents is applied in a more limited form than in the US. Under the Protocol on the Interpretation of Article 69 EPC (which the UK and HK follow), the extent of protection is determined by the claims, but the description and drawings may be used to interpret the claims. The doctrine of equivalents is applied to determine whether a variant falls within the scope of the claims. Under the Improver v. Remington [1990] FSR 181 test (UK), the court asks whether the variant has a material effect on the way the invention works, whether the skilled person would have known that the variant had no material effect, and whether the strict compliance with the literal meaning of the claim was intended. Under the Kirin-Amgen v. Hoechst Marion Roussel [2005] RPC 9 test (UK), the claims are interpreted by the skilled person in light of the specification. The doctrine of equivalents is less expansive in HK than in the US. The HK Patents Registry follows the UKIPO and EPO approach. The Patents Ordinance does not have a specific provision for the doctrine of equivalents.
notes: 三法域部分对应：CN法下（专利法第64条 + 司法实践）等同侵权广泛采用，'三基本一联想'标准；US法下（Warner-Jenkinson, Festo, 35 U.S.C. §271）doctrine of equivalents well-developed but limited by prosecution history estoppel；HK法下（Cap 514 + UK/EPO approach, Improver/Kirin-Amgen tests）doctrine of equivalents more limited。核心差异：US等同原则最发达，CN等同标准相对灵活，HK以UK/EPO limited equivalents approach为严格框架。hard_case=True。

fact_summary: 企业主张其未注册商标通过长期使用获得了显著性，要求禁止他人使用相同标识/已验证:unregistered trademark acquired distinctiveness partial
cn_claim: 依据《商标法》第11条，仅有本商品的通用名称、图形、型号的标志，或仅直接表示商品质量、主要原料、功能、用途、重量、数量及其他特点的标志，不得作为商标注册。但经过使用取得显著特征，并便于识别的，可以作为商标注册。对于未注册商标，若其已'有一定影响'，可依据反不正当竞争法第6条获得保护。
us_claim: Under US trademark law, the Lanham Act, 15 U.S.C. § 1052(f), provides that a mark that is merely descriptive may be registered if it has acquired distinctiveness through use in commerce. Under 15 U.S.C. § 1125(a), unregistered marks are protected against false designation of origin if they have acquired secondary meaning. The plaintiff must prove that the mark has acquired distinctiveness through extensive use and advertising. Under the Abercrombie & Fitch classification, marks are categorized as generic, descriptive, suggestive, arbitrary, or fanciful. Descriptive marks may be protected if they have acquired secondary meaning. Under the trademark use doctrine, the mark must be used in commerce to acquire rights. Under the common law, unregistered marks are protected in the geographic area of use. See the general principles of trademark distinctiveness and secondary meaning applied in US law. Under the Federal Circuit, the amount of evidence required to prove acquired distinctiveness varies depending on the descriptiveness of the mark.
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong trademark law, the Trade Marks Ordinance (Cap 559) provides for the registration of marks that have acquired distinctiveness through use. Under Section 15, a mark may be registered if it has acquired a distinctive character as a result of the use made of it. Under the common law tort of passing off, an unregistered mark may be protected if it has acquired goodwill (reputation) and the defendant's use is likely to cause confusion. The plaintiff must prove goodwill, misrepresentation, and damage. Under the Trade Marks Ordinance, the registration of a mark is not required for passing off protection. The common law of passing off is the primary remedy for unregistered marks in HK. The HK courts have recognized that a mark may acquire distinctiveness through extensive use and advertising. See the general principles of trademark distinctiveness and passing off applied in HK. The UK and EU case law on acquired distinctiveness (e.g., Philips v. Remington, C-299/99) is persuasive in HK. The Trade Marks Ordinance does not have a specific provision for the registration of unregistered marks, but the general provisions on acquired distinctiveness apply. The common law of passing off provides a robust remedy for unregistered marks with goodwill. See the general principles of trademark law and passing off applied in HK.
notes: 三法域部分对应：CN法下（商标法第11条 + 反不正当竞争法第6条）descriptive marks may acquire distinctiveness + unregistered marks with '有一定影响' protected；US法下（Lanham Act §1052(f), §1125(a)）descriptive marks may acquire distinctiveness + unregistered marks protected by secondary meaning；HK法下（Cap 559 §15 + passing off）descriptive marks may acquire distinctiveness + unregistered marks protected by goodwill/reputation。核心差异：三法域均承认使用获得的显著性，但US有最发达的secondary meaning doctrine，HK以passing off为未注册商标的主要保护途径，CN以反不正当竞争法补充。hard_case=True。

fact_summary: 著作权人主张其作品被用于搜索引擎索引，构成侵权/已验证:search engine indexing partial
cn_claim: 依据《著作权法》第10条、第24条，复制权、信息网络传播权属于著作权人专有权利。搜索引擎索引过程中复制作品可能构成复制，但搜索引擎通常主张其索引行为属于'合理使用'或'法定许可'。中国司法实践中，搜索引擎索引行为通常被视为对作品的'临时复制'或'合理使用'，但法院标准不一。
us_claim: Under US copyright law, 17 U.S.C. § 107, fair use is a defense to copyright infringement. Under the Google v. Oracle, 593 U.S. ___ (2021) decision, the Court held that Google's use of Oracle's Java API in the Android operating system was fair use. Under the Ninth Circuit in Kelly v. Arriba Soft, 336 F.3d 811 (9th Cir. 2003), the use of thumbnail images in a search engine was fair use. Under Perfect 10 v. Amazon, 508 F.3d 1146 (9th Cir. 2007), the use of thumbnail images in Google Image Search was fair use. Under the DMCA, 17 U.S.C. § 512, search engines may qualify for safe harbor if they comply with the notice-and-takedown procedure. Under the doctrine of transformative use, the search engine's use of copyrighted works for indexing may be fair use if it is transformative and does not substitute for the original work. The four fair use factors apply. See the general principles of fair use and search engine indexing applied in US law.
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong copyright law, the Copyright Ordinance (Cap 528) does not have a specific provision for search engine indexing. Under the fair dealing exceptions, Section 39(1) allows fair dealing for the purpose of research or private study; Section 39(4) for criticism or review; Section 39(5) for reporting current events. Search engine indexing may not fall within these statutory categories. Under the common law, the search engine's use of copyrighted works for indexing may be challenged as copyright infringement. The Copyright Ordinance does not have a general fair use provision. The search engine may rely on the safe harbor provisions in Section 88A for online service providers. The HK courts have not yet ruled on the copyright implications of search engine indexing. The UK and EU case law on caching and indexing (e.g., Infopaq v. Danske Dagblades Forening, C-5/08) is persuasive. The EU Directive 2001/29/EC (which does not apply in HK) provides exceptions for temporary acts of reproduction. The Copyright Ordinance does not have a specific exception for temporary reproduction. The search engine's liability for indexing is uncertain in HK. See the general principles of copyright law and the defenses applied in HK. The Copyright (Amendment) Ordinance 2020 did not introduce a specific exception for search engine indexing.
notes: 三法域部分对应：CN法下（著作权法第10条、第24条）搜索引擎索引可能构成合理使用，但标准不一；US法下（17 U.S.C. §107, Kelly v. Arriba Soft, Perfect 10 v. Amazon）search engine indexing generally fair use if transformative；HK法下（Cap 528 §39）fair dealing exceptions may not cover indexing，no specific case law。核心差异：US有明确判例支持搜索引擎索引的合理使用，CN司法实践尚在探索，HK缺乏专门判例。hard_case=True。

fact_summary: A Delaware corporation's charter contains an exculpation clause under DGCL §102(b)(7) eliminating director liability for duty of care breaches, while a Chinese subsidiary under PRC Company Law imposes strict personal liability on directors for company losses, and HK common law holds directors to a high standard of care with no statutory exculpation. / 一家特拉华州公司章程依据DGCL §102(b)(7)包含董事免责条款，免除违反注意义务的责任，而中国子公司依据《公司法》对董事造成公司损失施加严格个人责任，香港普通法对董事适用高标准的注意义务且无制定法免责。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第149条，董事执行公司职务时违反法律、行政法规或公司章程，给公司造成损失的，应当承担赔偿责任。中国法不承认董事注意义务的法定免责条款。
us_claim: Under DGCL §102(b)(7), a corporation may adopt a charter provision eliminating or limiting the personal liability of a director for monetary damages for breach of fiduciary duty as a director, provided it does not eliminate liability for breach of loyalty, acts not in good faith, or knowing violations of law. See also Smith v. Van Gorkom, 488 A.2d 858 (Del. 1985).
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong common law and the Companies Ordinance (Cap 622), directors owe fiduciary duties including duty of care, skill and diligence. There is no statutory equivalent to DGCL §102(b)(7); directors cannot contractually exculpate themselves from liability for negligence as a matter of public policy. See Re City Equitable Fire Insurance Co [1925] Ch 407.
notes: 三法域在董事注意义务责任上存在根本性冲突：美国特拉华州允许公司章程通过opt-in方式免除董事金钱赔偿责任（以鼓励商业冒险），中国法坚持董事对公司损失的严格个人赔偿责任（强调对公司和债权人保护），香港普通法保持传统高标准且不允许法定免责。这反映了英美法内部（特拉华vs普通法）以及成文法与普通法之间在公司治理哲学上的张力。

fact_summary: A shareholder files a derivative suit in the US without pre-suit demand, invoking demand futility under Aronson test, while Chinese law requires 90-day pre-litigation demand to the board with no exception, and HK derivative actions under Cap 622 require court leave under sections 732-733 with broad discretion. / 美国股东在未进行前置请求的情况下提起派生诉讼，援引Aronson测试下的demand futility规则，中国法要求向董事会进行90日前置请求且无例外，香港依据Cap 622第732-733条要求法院许可并享有广泛裁量权。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第151条，股东提起派生诉讼前必须书面请求监事会或董事会向人民法院提起诉讼，若其收到请求之日起三十日内未起诉，股东方可代表公司提起诉讼。中国法不存在demand futility例外。
us_claim: Under Delaware law, a shareholder must first make a demand on the board or plead particularized facts showing demand would be futile under the Aronson test (Aronson v. Lewis, 473 A.2d 805 (Del. 1984)). Demand is excused only if the board is interested or lacks independence.
hk_claim: Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) sections 732-733, a shareholder may bring a derivative action with leave of the court. The court considers whether the applicant is acting in good faith and whether the action is in the interests of the company. There is no strict pre-action demand requirement equivalent to PRC law, but the court has broad discretion to grant or refuse leave. See Waddington Ltd v. Chan Chun Hoo [2008] HKCU 1423.
notes: 三法域在股东派生诉讼前置程序上存在结构性冲突：美国特拉华法以demand futility规则允许在特定情况下跳过前置请求（基于董事会缺乏独立性），中国法采用绝对前置请求程序（30日等待期），香港法则以法院自由裁量的许可制度（leave制度）取代刚性前置程序。这反映了司法审查与行政前置程序在公司治理救济中的不同制度选择。

fact_summary: A company's material non-public information is selectively disclosed to analysts in the US triggering SEC Rule 10b-5 liability, while China Securities Law prohibits all insider trading by connected persons, and HK SFO Cap 571 prohibits disclosure of inside information to prevent market abuse. / 美国公司向分析师选择性披露重大非公开信息触发SEC Rule 10b-5责任，中国《证券法》禁止所有关联人内幕交易，香港《证券及期货条例》Cap 571禁止披露内幕信息以防止市场滥用。
cn_claim: 依据《证券法》第53条、第191条，证券交易内幕信息的知情人和非法获取内幕信息的人，在内幕信息公开前，不得买卖该证券或建议他人买卖。违反者承担民事赔偿责任、行政处罚及刑事责任。
us_claim: Under SEC Rule 10b-5 promulgated under Section 10(b) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, it is unlawful to use any manipulative or deceptive device in connection with the purchase or sale of securities, including trading on material non-public information or selective disclosure. See Chiarella v. United States, 445 U.S. 222 (1980); Dirks v. SEC, 463 U.S. 646 (1983).
hk_claim: Under Securities and Futures Ordinance (Cap 571) Part XIII, disclosure of inside information is prohibited to prevent market abuse. Section 245(1) prohibits a person who has information that he knows is relevant information in relation to a listed corporation from disclosing it to another person if he knows or has reasonable cause to believe that the other person will use it to deal in the listed securities.
notes: 三法域在内幕交易规制上存在冲突：美国法通过判例法（Chiarella/Dirks）发展出misappropriation和classical理论，强调fiduciary duty breach；中国法采用成文法禁止所有知情人交易，覆盖范围更广；香港法以披露为本位（disclosure-based）规制信息滥用。差异根源于美国判例法传统、中国集中立法模式与香港结合英国普通法和证券监管需求的混合模式。

fact_summary: A controlling shareholder engages in self-dealing transactions in the US where entire fairness review applies, while Chinese law requires shareholder approval for related-party transactions above thresholds, and HK Listing Rules require independent shareholders' approval for connected transactions. / 美国控股股东进行自我交易时适用entire fairness审查标准，中国法要求超过阈值的关联交易须经股东会批准，香港上市规则要求关连交易须经独立股东批准。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第182条及《上市公司信息披露管理办法》，公司与关联方发生的交易金额达到一定标准的，应当提交股东会审议。控股股东不得利用其关联关系损害公司利益，违反规定给公司造成损失的，应当承担赔偿责任。
us_claim: Under Delaware law, when a controlling shareholder engages in a self-dealing transaction, the transaction is subject to the entire fairness standard of review, requiring both fair dealing and fair price, unless the transaction is approved by fully informed, disinterested directors or disinterested shareholders. See Weinberger v. UOP, Inc., 457 A.2d 701 (Del. 1983).
hk_claim: Under the HKEX Listing Rules (Main Board Rule 14A), connected transactions require independent shareholders' approval and must be on normal commercial terms. The independent financial adviser must opine whether the terms are fair and reasonable. See also Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) section 461 regarding ratification of directors' breaches of duty.
notes: 三法域在关联交易审查标准上冲突：美国特拉华法采用司法主导的entire fairness实质审查（Weinberger），中国法采用以金额阈值为基础的股东会程序审批制，香港上市规则采用独立股东批准加独立财务顾问意见的制度。差异反映了美国司法审查传统、中国行政监管导向与香港交易所自律监管的多元治理路径。

fact_summary: A shareholder exercises appraisal rights in a US merger under DGCL §262, while Chinese Company Law allows dissenting shareholders to request repurchase in limited circumstances, and HK law does not provide statutory appraisal rights for mergers but allows unfair prejudice petitions under Cap 622 s.724. / 美国股东在合并中依据DGCL §262行使评估权，中国《公司法》仅在有限情形下允许异议股东请求回购，香港法未对合并提供制定法评估权但允许依据Cap 622第724条提起不公平损害呈请。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第89条、第161条，对股东会合并、分立决议投反对票的股东，可以请求公司按照合理的价格收购其股权。但此权利仅限于有限责任公司和特定情形下的股份有限公司，且程序要求严格。
us_claim: Under DGCL §262, a shareholder who dissents from a merger or consolidation has the right to seek appraisal and receive the fair value of their shares, excluding any element of value arising from the accomplishment of the merger. See also Weinberger v. UOP, Inc., 457 A.2d 701 (Del. 1983) (discussing fair value standard).
hk_claim: Hong Kong does not have a statutory appraisal rights regime for mergers equivalent to DGCL §262. However, under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) section 724, a shareholder may petition the court for relief on the grounds that the company's affairs are being conducted in a manner unfairly prejudicial to the members. See Re Guidezone [2000] 2 BCLC 321.
notes: 三法域在异议股东救济机制上冲突：美国特拉华法提供独立的评估权程序（appraisal remedy）作为现金退出机制，中国法仅在特定交易类型中提供回购请求权，香港法未设独立评估权但依赖不公平损害呈请（unfair prejudice petition）这一普通法救济。差异反映了美国法对股东退出权的重视、中国法对有限公司人合性的保护以及香港普通法传统对公司内部事务司法介入的审慎态度。

fact_summary: A target company's board adopts a poison pill rights plan in the US under Unocal review, while Chinese law does not recognize poison pills as a legitimate takeover defense, and HK Takeovers Code prohibits actions that frustrate a bona fide offer without shareholder approval. / 美国目标公司董事会在Unocal审查标准下采用毒丸计划作为收购防御，中国法不承认毒丸为合法的反收购措施，香港《收购守则》禁止未经股东批准挫败真诚要约的行为。
cn_claim: 中国《公司法》及《上市公司收购管理办法》未承认毒丸计划（poison pill）的合法性。中国上市公司不得擅自采取反收购措施。董事会在收购过程中应维护公司及全体股东利益，不得滥用职权对收购设置不适当障碍。
us_claim: Under Delaware law, a board may adopt defensive measures such as a poison pill (shareholder rights plan) if there is a reasonable threat to corporate policy and the response is proportionate and not preclusive or coercive. See Unocal Corp. v. Mesa Petroleum Co., 493 A.2d 946 (Del. 1985); Moran v. Household International, Inc., 500 A.2d 1346 (Del. 1985).
hk_claim: Under the Hong Kong Code on Takeovers and Mergers (Takeovers Code), Rule 4 restricts frustrating actions. The board of an offeree company must not take any action that could result in the frustration of a bona fide offer without the approval of shareholders in general meeting. Poison pills are generally not permitted as a defensive tactic. See also SEHK Listing Rule 10.06.
notes: 三法域在反收购防御措施上存在直接冲突：美国特拉华法在Unocal标准下允许符合比例原则的董事会防御（包括毒丸），中国法原则上禁止未经法定程序的反收购措施，香港《收购守则》以股东批准为前提限制挫败行动。差异反映了美国董事会在公司政治中的中心地位、中国法对收购效率的偏好以及香港对中小股东保护的监管优先性。

fact_summary: A corporation is required to indemnify directors for litigation expenses under DGCL §145, while Chinese law requires directors to bear personal liability for company debts in certain insolvency situations, and HK law allows indemnity only for negligence (not fraud or dishonesty) under Cap 622 s.469. / 美国公司依据DGCL §145被要求补偿董事诉讼费用，中国法要求董事在特定破产情形下对公司债务承担个人责任，香港法依据Cap 622第469条仅允许对过失（非欺诈或不诚实）进行补偿。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第191条及《企业破产法》第125条，企业董事、监事或者高级管理人员违反忠实义务、勤勉义务，致使所在企业破产的，依法承担民事责任。此外，若公司无法清偿债务且董事存在过错，董事可能须对公司债务承担连带责任。
us_claim: Under DGCL §145, a corporation has the power to indemnify any person who was or is a party to any proceeding by reason of the fact that he is or was a director, officer, employee or agent of the corporation, against expenses actually and reasonably incurred in connection with the defense, provided he acted in good faith and in a manner reasonably believed to be in the best interests of the corporation.
hk_claim: Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) section 469, a company may indemnify a director against liability incurred in defending proceedings where judgment is given in his favor or he is acquitted, but indemnity against negligence, default, breach of duty or breach of trust is permitted only if the director acted honestly and reasonably. Fraud and dishonesty are excluded. See also Cap 622 s.470.
notes: 三法域在董事补偿与责任承担上冲突：美国特拉华法允许广泛补偿（indemnification）以鼓励董事服务，中国法在破产情形下对董事施加个人责任以遏制过度冒险，香港法允许对过失的补偿但排除欺诈和不诚实。差异反映了美国法对董事的风险分担机制、中国法对债权人的优先保护以及香港法在诚实与合理标准之间的平衡。

fact_summary: US corporate law recognizes the corporate opportunity doctrine strictly (Meinhard v. Salmon), while Chinese law prohibits directors from usurping company opportunities under Company Law Art. 183, and HK law applies the common law rule that directors must not place themselves in a conflict of interest. / 美国公司法严格承认公司机会原则（Meinhard v. Salmon），中国《公司法》第183条禁止董事篡夺公司机会，香港普通法适用董事不得使自己陷入利益冲突的规则。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第183条，董事、监事、高级管理人员不得利用职权谋取属于公司的商业机会。董事违反前款规定所得的收入应当归公司所有。此规则不以公司不能利用该机会为前提，构成严格禁止。
us_claim: Under Delaware law, the corporate opportunity doctrine requires that a director or officer may not take a business opportunity for his own if: (1) the corporation is financially able to exploit it; (2) the opportunity is within the corporation's line of business; (3) the corporation has an interest or expectancy in it; and (4) the officer/director took it in an individual capacity. See Guth v. Loft, 5 A.2d 503 (Del. 1939); Meinhard v. Salmon, 164 N.E. 545 (N.Y. 1928).
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong common law, directors owe a fiduciary duty not to place themselves in a position where their personal interests conflict with their duties to the company. The corporate opportunity doctrine is recognized: a director must not usurp a corporate opportunity without the company's informed consent. See Regal (Hastings) Ltd v. Gulliver [1942] 1 All ER 378; Bhullar v. Bhullar [2003] EWCA Civ 424.
notes: 三法域在公司机会原则上存在冲突：美国法采用四要素测试（Guth标准）要求公司具备财务能力和业务相关性，中国法采取绝对禁止态度（不以公司能力为前提），香港普通法遵循Regal标准强调严格受托责任。差异反映了美国法对商业效率的考量、中国法对董事机会主义的零容忍以及英国普通法传统对fiduciary duty的严格解释。

fact_summary: Dual-class share structures are permitted in US (e.g., Google, Meta) and HK (with weighted voting right limits), but prohibited for general PRC companies unless under STAR Market with strict conditions. / 双重股权结构在美国（如Google、Meta）和香港（受限制加权投票权）允许，但中国一般公司禁止，仅在科创板适用严格条件。
cn_claim: 中国《公司法》第143条规定，股份有限公司发行股份实行同股同权原则。中国一般公司禁止发行具有特别表决权的股份。科创板（STAR Market）允许同股不同权架构，但须满足严格的市值、持股期限及投票权倍数限制（《科创板上市规则》4.5.1）。
us_claim: Under US corporate law and stock exchange rules (NYSE/Nasdaq), dual-class share structures are generally permitted, allowing founders to maintain control through superior voting rights. See Google Inc. IPO (2004) with Class A (1 vote) and Class B (10 votes). The SEC and exchanges have periodically debated sunset provisions but not banned them. See Council of Institutional Investors v. SEC, 970 F.3d 388 (D.C. Cir. 2020).
hk_claim: Under HKEX Listing Rules (Main Board), Chapter 8A permits weighted voting right (WVR) structures for eligible issuers, subject to stringent requirements: minimum market cap of HK$40 billion, WVR beneficiaries must be directors with substantial contribution, and WVR shares are limited to 10 votes per share. See HKEX Listing Rules 8A.09-8A.24.
notes: 三法域不对称：美国和香港允许双重股权结构（美国更宽松，香港有严格限制），而中国一般公司法禁止同股不同权（仅科创板例外）。差异源于美国法对创始人控制的宽容、香港在吸引科技企业与保护投资者之间的平衡，以及中国法对shareholder democracy的优先选择。

fact_summary: Directors and Officers (D&O) liability insurance is standard in US public companies, increasingly available in HK, but still emerging and limited in China due to regulatory and cultural barriers. / 董事及高管责任保险在美国上市公司是标准配置，在香港日益普及，但在中国因监管和文化障碍仍处于新兴且受限阶段。
cn_claim: 中国《公司法》未明确禁止董事责任保险，但实践中D&O保险在中国A股市场仍属新兴产品。2020年《证券法》修订后强化了董事责任，推动了D&O保险需求，但监管对保险范围（如是否覆盖行政处罚）存在限制。
us_claim: D&O insurance is a standard component of US corporate governance, typically covering directors and officers for claims arising from alleged wrongful acts in their capacity as corporate leaders. Under Delaware law, DGCL §145 permits a corporation to purchase insurance on behalf of officers and directors. See also In re The Dow Chemical Company Derivative Litigation, 2021 WL 1458100 (Del. Ch. 2021).
hk_claim: D&O insurance is increasingly common for Hong Kong listed companies under HKEX Listing Rules and market practice. While not statutorily mandated, it is strongly encouraged by institutional investors and the Hong Kong Institute of Directors as part of good governance. The insurance market in HK is well-developed following Lloyd's and London market traditions.
notes: 三法域不对称：美国D&O保险是标准治理工具（由成熟的保险市场和判例法支持），香港保险市场成熟但非强制，中国D&O保险因监管不确定性、诉讼文化不发达和公司法传统而发展滞后。差异反映了不同法域的诉讼风险环境、保险市场成熟度以及对董事保护的文化态度。

fact_summary: SPAC (Special Purpose Acquisition Company) listings flourished in the US (Nasdaq/NYSE) with mature rules, were permitted in HK with stricter investor protection requirements, but remain prohibited in mainland China A-share market. / SPAC（特殊目的收购公司）在美国（纳斯达克/纽交所）规则成熟并繁荣，在香港允许但投资者保护要求更严格，但在境内A股市场仍被禁止。
cn_claim: 中国证券监管机构（证监会）目前未允许SPAC在A股市场上市。中国IPO制度仍以审核制/注册制下的实质审查为主，SPAC模式与现行《证券法》和上市规则的合并程序、信息披露要求存在制度冲突。科创板和创业板亦未引入SPAC机制。
us_claim: Under SEC rules and Nasdaq/NYSE listing standards, SPACs are a well-established vehicle for going public. The SEC has adopted specific disclosure requirements for SPACs, including financial projections and redemption rights. See SEC Release No. 34-92586 (2021); In re MultiPlan Corp. Stockholders Litigation, 2022 WL 2406250 (Del. Ch. 2022).
hk_claim: Under HKEX guidance (2022) and amended Listing Rules (Main Board), SPAC listings are permitted subject to stringent conditions: minimum IPO size of HK$1 billion, only professional investors may participate in the SPAC stage, and the de-SPAC transaction requires independent shareholder approval and a PIPE investment. See HKEX Listing Rules 18B.01-18B.69.
notes: 三法域不对称：美国拥有最成熟的SPAC市场和监管框架，香港在2022年引入但限制更多（仅限专业投资者、更高门槛），中国A股完全禁止SPAC。差异源于美国对资本市场创新的宽容、香港对散户投资者保护的审慎以及中国对直接上市模式的路径依赖和监管保守主义。

fact_summary: US corporate law prioritizes shareholder primacy (Dodge v. Ford), while UK-influenced HK law recognizes stakeholder interests under common law and listing rules, and Chinese Company Law explicitly mandates corporate social responsibility. / 美国公司法优先股东至上（Dodge v. Ford），受英国影响的香港普通法和上市规则承认利益相关者利益，中国《公司法》明确强制企业社会责任。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第19条、第20条，公司从事经营活动应遵守社会公德、商业道德，承担社会责任。2023年修订的《公司法》进一步强化了对职工、债权人等利益相关者的保护，并明确公司需考虑生态环境保护等社会公共利益。
us_claim: Under Delaware law, the traditional standard is that a corporation's purpose is to maximize shareholder value. In Dodge v. Ford Motor Co., 170 N.W. 668 (Mich. 1919), the court held that a business corporation is organized and carried on primarily for the profit of the stockholders. While recent developments (B Corp legislation, constituency statutes) allow some stakeholder consideration, shareholder primacy remains the dominant doctrine. See eBay Domestic Holdings v. Newmark, 16 A.3d 1 (Del. Ch. 2010).
hk_claim: Hong Kong corporate law, following UK common law tradition, does not strictly enforce shareholder primacy. While directors owe duties to the company (interpreted as shareholders collectively), the Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) and HKEX Listing Rules encourage consideration of stakeholder interests including employees, creditors, and the community. See Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) section 126 (statement of company's objects may include social purposes).
notes: 三法域不对称：美国坚持股东至上（Dodge v. Ford），中国公司法明确写入社会责任条款，香港处于中间位置（普通法传统允许更广泛的利益考量）。差异反映了美国资本主义的原教旨主义、中国社会主义法律传统对公司社会角色的要求，以及英国普通法在股东与利益相关者之间的平衡立场。

fact_summary: A company is incorporated by filing constitutional documents with the registrar in all three jurisdictions, requiring minimum shareholder(s), directors, and a registered office. / 在三法域均通过向注册处提交章程文件设立公司，均要求最低股东人数、董事及注册地址。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第29条、第46条，设立公司应当依法向公司登记机关申请设立登记，提交公司章程、股东身份证明等文件。有限责任公司由五十个以下股东出资设立。
us_claim: Under DGCL §101, a corporation is formed by filing a certificate of incorporation with the Delaware Secretary of State. The certificate must include the name, address of registered office, purpose, authorized stock, and incorporator information. See also DGCL §102.
hk_claim: Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) sections 67-102, a company is formed by delivering incorporation documents (articles of association, notice of registered office) to the Companies Registry. A private company limited by shares must have at least one director (who must be a natural person) and one member. See Cap 622 s.453-454.
notes: 三法域映射：公司设立的基本要素（注册文件、最低股东/董事、注册地址）在三法域高度同构。中国法、美国特拉华法和香港法均采用注册成立原则（incorporation doctrine），差异仅在于程序细节（如中国要求验资/认缴，特拉华要求registered agent）。这是全球化公司法趋同（convergence）的典型例证。

fact_summary: Shareholders in all three jurisdictions have the right to inspect corporate books and records, subject to proper purpose requirements. / 在三法域股东均有权查阅公司账簿和记录，但须符合正当目的要求。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第57条、第110条，股东有权查阅、复制公司章程、股东名册、财务会计报告等。股东要求查阅公司会计账簿、会计凭证的，应当向公司提出书面请求，说明目的。公司有合理根据认为股东查阅有不正当目的，可能损害公司合法利益的，可以拒绝提供查阅。
us_claim: Under DGCL §220, any stockholder has the right to inspect the corporation's stock ledger, books and records, and a list of its stockholders upon written demand under oath stating a proper purpose. The proper purpose standard is broadly construed to include investigating potential wrongdoing. See Seinfeld v. Verizon, 909 A.2d 117 (Del. 2006).
hk_claim: Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) section 657, a member of a company has the right to inspect the company's records of resolutions, minutes of general meetings, and the register of members. Under common law, shareholders may also seek inspection of books and records for a proper purpose. See Re Scottish Co-operative Wholesale Society Ltd [1959] SC 53.
notes: 三法域映射：股东知情权（inspection rights）在三法域均以'正当目的'（proper purpose）为限制条件，构成基本的公司治理机制。美国特拉华法（DGCL §220）和中国法（Company Law Art. 57）均要求书面请求+目的说明，香港法则结合制定法（Cap 622 s.657）和普通法。制度同构性源于股东作为公司所有者的信息需求。

fact_summary: Directors in all three jurisdictions owe fiduciary duties of loyalty and care to the corporation, enforceable by shareholders and the company. / 在三法域董事均对公司负有忠实义务和注意义务的受托责任，可由股东和公司强制执行。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第180条、第181条，董事、监事、高级管理人员应当遵守法律、行政法规和公司章程，对公司负有忠实义务和勤勉义务。董事不得利用职权收受贿赂或其他非法收入，不得侵占公司财产。
us_claim: Under Delaware law, directors owe fiduciary duties of care and loyalty to the corporation and its shareholders. The duty of loyalty requires that directors act in the best interests of the corporation, avoiding self-dealing and usurping corporate opportunities. The duty of care requires informed decision-making. See Aronson v. Lewis, 473 A.2d 805 (Del. 1984); Revlon, Inc. v. MacAndrews & Forbes Holdings, Inc., 506 A.2d 173 (Del. 1986).
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong common law and Companies Ordinance (Cap 622), directors owe fiduciary duties of care, skill and diligence, and loyalty to the company. These duties are enforceable by the company and, in derivative actions, by shareholders. See Re City Equitable Fire Insurance Co [1925] Ch 407; Regal (Hastings) Ltd v. Gulliver [1942] 1 All ER 378.
notes: 三法域映射：董事受托责任（fiduciary duties of care and loyalty）是三法域公司法的共同基石。中国法第180条明确写入忠实义务和勤勉义务，美国特拉华判例法（Aronson/Revlon）将其发展为系统的审查标准，香港普通法（Regal标准）同样严格。这是公司法全球化的核心共识，尽管审查标准（如商业判断规则）的严格程度存在差异。

fact_summary: A company may be dissolved and liquidated through a formal winding-up process in all three jurisdictions, with creditors and shareholders participating in the distribution of assets. / 在三法域公司均可通过正式清盘程序解散，债权人及股东参与资产分配。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第229条、第235条，公司因章程规定、股东会决议、合并分立、被吊销执照等原因解散，应当在解散事由出现之日起十五日内成立清算组。清算组清理公司财产，编制资产负债表和财产清单，按顺序支付清算费用、职工工资、社会保险费用、法定补偿金、税款、债务后，剩余财产分配给股东。
us_claim: Under Delaware law, a corporation may be dissolved voluntarily by a vote of the stockholders or involuntarily by the Court of Chancery. Under DGCL §280-281, a dissolved corporation must wind up its affairs, pay or make provision for payment of claims, and distribute remaining assets to stockholders. Creditors have priority over shareholders. See North American Catholic Educational Programming Foundation v. Gheewalla, 930 A.2d 92 (Del. 2007).
hk_claim: Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) Part V and Companies (Winding Up and Miscellaneous Provisions) Ordinance (Cap 32), a company may be wound up voluntarily (members' or creditors' winding up) or compulsorily by the court. Assets are distributed according to the statutory waterfall: costs, preferential creditors, secured creditors, unsecured creditors, and then shareholders. See Cap 32 s.170-178.
notes: 三法域映射：公司解散与清算制度在三法域均遵循相似的法定顺序（清算费用→职工债权→税款→普通债权→股东）。中国《公司法》第235条、美国DGCL §280-281、香港Cap 32第170-178条均建立了债权人优先于股东的分配规则。这反映了破产法与公司法在全球化背景下的功能趋同。

fact_summary: Related-party transactions require disclosure and/or approval by disinterested parties in all three jurisdictions to prevent conflicts of interest. / 在三法域关联交易均要求披露和/或无利害关系方批准，以防止利益冲突。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第182条，公司的控股股东、实际控制人、董事、监事、高级管理人员不得利用其关联关系损害公司利益。违反前款规定，给公司造成损失的，应当承担赔偿责任。上市公司关联交易须符合《上市公司信息披露管理办法》和交易所规则，达到一定金额须经股东会批准。
us_claim: Under Delaware law and SEC rules, related-party transactions must be disclosed and, for material transactions, approved by disinterested directors or shareholders. Under SEC Regulation S-K Item 404, public companies must disclose transactions exceeding $120,000 in which a related person has a direct or indirect material interest. See also In re Oracle Corp. Derivative Litigation, 824 A.2d 917 (Del. 2003).
hk_claim: Under HKEX Listing Rules (Main Board Rules 14A.34-14A.60), connected transactions must be disclosed, subject to independent shareholders' approval, and supported by an independent financial adviser's opinion. Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) section 534, directors must disclose material interests in transactions. See HKEX Listing Rules 14A.36.
notes: 三法域映射：关联交易规制在三法域均围绕'披露+批准'双轨制展开。中国法（Company Law Art. 182）禁止损害公司利益的关联交易，美国特拉华法/SEC规则（Reg S-K Item 404）要求披露和独立批准，香港上市规则（Rules 14A.34-14A.60）要求独立股东批准和独立财务顾问意见。三者均试图通过程序正义（disinterested approval）解决控股股东代理问题。

fact_summary: A corporation may enter into a merger or consolidation with another company in all three jurisdictions, subject to shareholder approval and statutory procedures. / 在三法域公司均可与另一公司合并或整合，但须经股东批准和遵循法定程序。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第218条、第219条，公司合并应当由合并各方签订合并协议，编制资产负债表及财产清单。公司合并，应当由合并各方股东会作出决议。公司合并时，合并各方的债权、债务，应当由合并后存续的公司或者新设的公司承继。
us_claim: Under DGCL §251-252, a merger or consolidation requires a board resolution and approval by a majority of the outstanding stock entitled to vote. The surviving corporation succeeds to all assets, rights, and liabilities of the merged corporations. See DGCL §259; Paramount Communications v. QVC Network, 637 A.2d 34 (Del. 1994).
hk_claim: Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) sections 678-699, a company may merge with one or more companies by way of a court-approved scheme of arrangement or a statutory merger. The merger requires shareholder approval by special resolution (75% majority) and court sanction. See Cap 622 s.678; Re Hawk Insurance Co Ltd [2001] EWCA Civ 241.
notes: 三法域映射：公司合并制度在三法域均要求股东会批准（通常特别决议）、债权人保护程序及债务自动承继。中国法（Company Law Art. 218-219）要求合并协议和资产负债表，美国特拉华法（DGCL §251-252）要求董事会和股东批准，香港法（Cap 622 s.678-699）要求法院批准的计划安排（scheme of arrangement）。这是公司法组织重组规则的国际趋同。

fact_summary: Companies must maintain a register of members/shareholders and update it upon changes in all three jurisdictions. / 在三法域公司均须维持股东名册并在变更时更新。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第86条、第87条，有限责任公司应当置备股东名册，记载股东姓名、住所、出资额、出资证明书编号等。股东转让股权后，公司应当注销原股东的出资证明书，向新股东签发出资证明书，并相应修改公司章程和股东名册。
us_claim: Under DGCL §219, every corporation shall keep a stock ledger containing the name and address of each stockholder, the number and class of shares held, and the dates of issuance and transfer. The ledger is the prima facie evidence of stock ownership. See also DGCL §158 (certificate of stock).
hk_claim: Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) sections 627-635, a company must keep a register of members containing the name, address, date of becoming/ceasing to be a member, and shares held. The register must be updated within 2 months of any change. See Cap 622 s.627(1).
notes: 三法域映射：股东名册（register of members）是公司法的基础设施，在三法域均要求持续维护并记录股东身份、持股及变更信息。中国法（Company Law Art. 86-87）、美国特拉华法（DGCL §219）和香港法（Cap 622 s.627-635）均将其作为股东权利和公司治理的基石。制度同构源于股权确权的共同需求。

fact_summary: Directors must disclose material personal interests in company transactions to avoid conflicts in all three jurisdictions. / 在三法域董事均须披露在交易中的重大个人利益以避免利益冲突。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第182条、第185条，董事、监事、高级管理人员直接或间接与公司订立合同或进行交易，应当就有关事项向董事会或股东会报告，并按公司章程规定经董事会或股东会决议通过。董事违反本法规定所得的收入应当归公司所有。
us_claim: Under Delaware law, a director must disclose all material facts regarding a personal interest in a transaction to the board or shareholders. Under DGCL §144, a contract or transaction in which a director has a financial interest is not voidable if the material facts are disclosed and the board or shareholders approve it in good faith. See Marciano v. Nakash, 535 A.2d 400 (Del. 1987).
hk_claim: Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) section 534, a director who has a material interest in a transaction with the company must declare the nature and extent of that interest to the other directors. Failure to disclose may result in the transaction being voidable and the director being liable for breach of fiduciary duty. See Cap 622 s.534(1).
notes: 三法域映射：董事利益冲突披露义务在三法域均以'向董事会/股东披露+回避表决'为核心机制。中国法（Company Law Art. 182, 185）要求报告和股东会/董事会批准，美国特拉华法（DGCL §144）以disclosure+approval为安全港，香港法（Cap 622 s.534）要求向其他董事披露。三者均试图通过透明度（transparency）解决代理问题。

fact_summary: Corporate financial statements must be audited by independent auditors annually in all three jurisdictions for public companies. / 在三法域上市公司均须由独立审计师年度审计公司财务报表。
cn_claim: 依据《证券法》第160条、第180条，公开发行证券的公司应当依法经会计师事务所审计。公司聘用、解聘承办公司审计业务的会计师事务所，按照公司章程的规定，由股东会、董事会或监事会决定。审计报告应当由注册会计师签名并盖章。
us_claim: Under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 Section 10A and Sarbanes-Oxley Act Section 103, public companies must file audited annual financial statements with the SEC. The independent auditor must be a registered public accounting firm (PCAOB-registered) and must comply with PCAOB auditing standards. See 15 U.S.C. §78j-1.
hk_claim: Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) sections 405-409 and HKEX Listing Rules, every public company must appoint an auditor at each annual general meeting to hold office until the next AGM. The auditor must be a certified public accountant (practising) registered under the Professional Accountants Ordinance (Cap 50). See Cap 622 s.405(1).
notes: 三法域映射：独立审计制度是三法域证券监管的共同基石。中国法（Securities Law Art. 160, 180）要求公开发行公司经审计，美国法（Exchange Act §10A/SOX §103）要求PCAOB注册审计师，香港法（Cap 622 s.405-409）要求注册会计师。三者均通过独立审计（independent audit）机制解决信息不对称，体现了国际证券监管趋同（IOSCO原则）。

fact_summary: Shareholders may remove directors by ordinary resolution in all three jurisdictions, subject to notice and procedural requirements. / 在三法域股东均可以普通决议罢免董事，但须遵守通知和程序要求。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第71条、第116条，股东会可以决议解除董事职务，决议作出之日解任生效。无正当理由，在任期届满前解除董事职务的，公司应当向该董事作出补偿。股东会会议通知应当包括拟解除董事职务的内容。
us_claim: Under DGCL §141(k), any director or the entire board may be removed, with or without cause, by the holders of a majority of the shares then entitled to vote at an election of directors. The certificate of incorporation may require a supermajority vote or limit removal to cause only. See Campbell v. Loew's, Inc., 134 A.2d 852 (Del. Ch. 1957).
hk_claim: Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) section 462, a company may by ordinary resolution at a general meeting remove a director before the expiration of the director's term, notwithstanding anything in the company's articles or any agreement between the company and the director. Special notice (28 days) is required. See Cap 622 s.462(1); Bushell v. Faith [1970] AC 1099.
notes: 三法域映射：股东罢免董事权（removal of directors）是三法域公司法民主性的共同体现。中国法（Company Law Art. 71, 116）允许股东会无理由罢免但可能需补偿，美国特拉华法（DGCL §141(k)）允许无因罢免（除非章程限制），香港法（Cap 622 s.462）允许特别通知下的罢免。三者均确认股东会作为最高权力机关对董事的控制权。

fact_summary: Cumulative voting for director elections is permitted in some US states and for Chinese joint-stock companies under certain conditions, but not available in Hong Kong's standard one-share-one-vote system. / 累积投票制在美国部分州和中国股份有限公司特定条件下允许，但在香港标准的一股一票制度下不可用。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第117条，股份有限公司选举董事、监事，可以按照公司章程的规定或股东会的决议，实行累积投票制。累积投票制是指股东会选举董事或监事时，每一股份拥有与应选董事或监事人数相同的表决权，股东拥有的表决权可以集中使用。
us_claim: Cumulative voting is permitted in some US states (e.g., California Corporations Code §708 for non-public companies) but not in Delaware. Under California law, shareholders may cumulate their votes by giving one candidate a number of votes equal to the number of directors to be elected multiplied by the number of shares held. See California Corp. Code §708.
hk_claim: Hong Kong does not provide for cumulative voting under the standard Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) or HKEX Listing Rules. The default rule is one-share-one-vote per resolution (straight voting). Cumulative voting is not a recognized feature of Hong Kong corporate law, though companies could theoretically adopt it by special articles. See Cap 622 s.114 (voting rights determined by articles).
notes: 三法域部分映射：累积投票制（cumulative voting）在美国部分州（如加州）和中国股份有限公司（Company Law Art. 117）允许，但特拉华州和香港不支持。中国法将其作为保护中小股东的可选机制，美国法在州层面分裂，香港法遵循英国传统的一股一票。差异反映了不同法域对少数股东保护机制的路径选择：累积投票 vs 独立董事会 vs 不公平损害呈请。

fact_summary: Shareholder derivative actions are available in all three jurisdictions but with fundamentally different procedural requirements and standing thresholds. / 股东派生诉讼在三法域均可用，但程序要求和原告资格门槛根本不同。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第151条，有限责任公司的股东、股份有限公司连续一百八十日以上单独或者合计持有公司百分之一以上股份的股东，可以书面请求监事会或董事会向人民法院提起诉讼。监事会或董事会拒绝提起诉讼，或者三十日内未提起诉讼，股东有权为公司利益以自己的名义直接向人民法院提起诉讼。
us_claim: Under Delaware law, a shareholder bringing a derivative action must either make a demand on the board or plead demand futility under the Aronson test. The plaintiff must have been a stockholder at the time of the wrongdoing (contemporaneous ownership rule) and must fairly and adequately represent the interests of stockholders. See Aronson v. Lewis, 473 A.2d 805 (Del. 1984).
hk_claim: Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) sections 732-737, a shareholder may apply for court leave to bring a derivative action. The court must be satisfied that: (a) the applicant is acting in good faith; (b) it is in the company's interests; and (c) the application is made with the leave of the court. There is no strict ownership percentage threshold or pre-action demand rule. See Waddington Ltd v. Chan Chun Hoo [2008] HKCU 1423.
notes: 三法域部分映射：虽然三法域均提供派生诉讼（derivative action），但程序机制根本不同。中国法要求180日持股+1%门槛+30日前置请求，美国特拉华法要求demand或demand futility+contemporaneous ownership，香港法以法院自由裁量许可（leave）为核心。这是典型的'制度功能等效但结构差异'（functional equivalence with structural divergence）。

fact_summary: Piercing the corporate veil is recognized in all three jurisdictions but applied under different standards—US uses alter ego/instrumentality tests, China uses personality混同, and HK follows UK common law fraud/window dressing tests. / 揭开公司面纱在三法域均被承认，但适用标准不同：美国采用alter ego/工具性测试，中国采用人格混同，香港遵循英国普通法欺诈/粉饰测试。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第23条，公司股东滥用公司法人独立地位和股东有限责任，逃避债务，严重损害公司债权人利益的，应当对公司债务承担连带责任。司法实践中，法院通常审查是否存在人格混同（财产混同、业务混同、人员混同、场所混同）。参见《全国法院民商事审判工作会议纪要》（九民纪要）第10-12条。
us_claim: Under US law, courts may pierce the corporate veil under the alter ego doctrine, instrumentality rule, or fraud test. The key factors include: failure to observe corporate formalities, undercapitalization, commingling of funds, and dominance by a parent company. See United States v. Milwaukee Refrigerator Transit Co., 142 F.247 (C.C.E.D. Wis. 1905); Sea-Land Services v. Pepper Source, 941 F.2d 519 (7th Cir. 1991).
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong common law, the corporate veil may be pierced in cases of fraud, sham or façade, or where a company is the agent of its controller. The courts are generally reluctant to pierce the veil and require clear evidence that the company was used as a vehicle for improper conduct. See Prest v. Petrodel Resources Ltd [2013] UKSC 34; Re A Company [1985] BCLC 333.
notes: 三法域部分映射：揭开公司面纱（piercing the corporate veil）是三法域公司法的基本原则例外，但测试标准根本不同。美国法发展出alter ego和instrumentality多因素测试（Sea-Land），中国法通过九民纪要确立人格混同标准（财产/业务/人员混同），香港法遵循英国Supreme Court在Prest案中设定的严格欺诈标准。差异反映了普通法与大陆法在实体穿透上的方法论分歧。

fact_summary: Corporate social responsibility (CSR) and ESG disclosure are mandatory in China for certain industries, encouraged in the US through SEC climate disclosure rules, and required for HK listed companies under the ESG Reporting Guide. / 企业社会责任和ESG披露在中国对特定行业强制，在美国通过SEC气候披露规则鼓励，在香港对上市公司依据ESG报告指引要求。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第19条、第20条，公司从事经营活动应遵守社会公德、商业道德，承担社会责任。上海证券交易所和深圳证券交易所要求特定上市公司（如科创板、重污染行业）披露ESG/社会责任报告。2024年三大交易所发布《上市公司可持续发展报告指引》，要求特定上市公司强制披露可持续发展报告。
us_claim: Under SEC rules (2024 Climate Disclosure Rules, 17 CFR Parts 232, 239, 240, 249), public companies are required to disclose material climate-related risks and greenhouse gas emissions, including Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions. The rules are based on materiality principles under the Securities Act of 1933 and Exchange Act of 1934. See SEC Release No. 33-11275 (2024). Note: litigation challenging the rules is pending. PENDING_VERIFICATION on final enforceability.
hk_claim: Under HKEX Listing Rules Appendix C2 (ESG Reporting Guide), all listed companies must publish an ESG report annually covering environmental, social, and governance aspects. The reporting is on a 'comply or explain' basis, with certain mandatory disclosure requirements. See HKEX Listing Rules Appendix C2, ESG Reporting Guide (2023 update).
notes: 三法域部分映射：ESG/CSR披露在三法域均呈上升趋势，但制度基础不同。中国法以交易所规则和《公司法》社会责任条款为强制基础（2024年《可持续发展报告指引》），美国法以SEC materiality-based气候披露规则为框架（正面临诉讼挑战），香港法以交易所'comply or explain'自律规则为机制。差异反映了行政监管、证券监管与交易所自律的多元治理模式。

fact_summary: Staggered board provisions are permitted in the US (anti-takeover defense), generally unavailable in China, and theoretically possible but uncommon in HK. / 交错董事会条款在美国允许（反收购防御），在中国一般不可用，在香港理论上可能但不常见。
cn_claim: 中国《公司法》未规定交错董事会制度。中国公司董事任期通常为三年，股东会可随时罢免董事（无因解除），因此交错董事会难以作为反收购防御措施存在。上市公司章程指引亦未引入交错董事会条款。
us_claim: Under Delaware law, a corporation may adopt a staggered (classified) board in its certificate of incorporation, dividing directors into classes with multi-year terms. This is a common anti-takeover defense because it prevents a hostile bidder from gaining control of the board in a single election. See DGCL §141(d); Air Products v. Airgas, 16 A.3d 48 (Del. Ch. 2011).
hk_claim: Under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622), there is no statutory prohibition on staggered boards, but it is uncommon in Hong Kong practice. The UK Corporate Governance Code discourages staggered boards, and HK listed companies generally follow unitary board structures with annual re-election of directors. See HKEX Corporate Governance Code D.1.2 (all directors subject to retirement by rotation at least every three years).
notes: 三法域部分映射：交错董事会（staggered board）在美国特拉华法（DGCL §141(d)）是合法反收购工具，在中国法不存在制度基础（董事可随时罢免），在香港虽无禁止但受公司治理准则 discouragement。差异反映了美国法对董事会防御的宽容、中国法对股东民主的坚持以及香港对英国单一董事会传统的遵循。

fact_summary: Beneficial ownership disclosure is required in all three jurisdictions but with different thresholds and enforcement mechanisms—US uses Section 13(d) (5%), China uses 5% disclosure plus detailed change reports, and HK uses SFO disclosure of interests (5%). / 受益所有权披露在三法域均要求，但阈值和执法机制不同：美国使用《证券交易法》第13(d)条（5%），中国使用5%披露加详细变动报告，香港使用《证券及期货条例》权益披露（5%）。
cn_claim: 依据《证券法》第63条、第65条，通过证券交易所的证券交易，投资者持有或者通过协议、其他安排与他人共同持有一个上市公司已发行的有表决权股份达到百分之五时，应当在该事实发生之日起三日内，向国务院证券监督管理机构、证券交易所作出书面报告。此后每增减1%或5%均需披露。
us_claim: Under Section 13(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and SEC Rule 13d-1, any person who acquires beneficial ownership of more than 5% of a class of registered equity securities must file a Schedule 13D within 10 days. The disclosure must include the purchaser's identity, source of funds, and purpose of the transaction. See 15 U.S.C. §78m(d); SEC v. Vertex Fund Management Ltd, 2015 WL 1379709 (S.D.N.Y. 2015).
hk_claim: Under Securities and Futures Ordinance (Cap 571) Part XV, a person who acquires or ceases to have a notifiable interest (5% or more) in a listed corporation must disclose the interest to the company and the Stock Exchange. Substantial shareholders must file disclosure of interests forms (Forms 1, 2, 3). See Cap 571 s.310-313; SFC Disclosure of Interests Guidelines.
notes: 三法域部分映射：5%权益披露制度在三法域均存在（中国Securities Law Art. 63、美国Exchange Act §13(d)、香港SFO Part XV），但触发后的报告义务不同。美国要求10日内Schedule 13D，中国要求3日内书面报告且后续1%/5%变动均需披露，香港要求向公司和联交所披露。差异反映了监管强度与信息效率之间的不同平衡。

fact_summary: Independent directors are required for public companies in all three jurisdictions but with different independence standards, ratios, and roles. / 独立董事在三法域均要求上市公司设立，但独立性标准、比例和角色不同。
cn_claim: 依据《公司法》第136条，上市公司设独立董事，具体管理办法由国务院证券监督管理机构规定。中国证监会《上市公司独立董事管理办法》要求独立董事占董事会成员比例不低于三分之一，且须独立于公司、主要股东和实际控制人。独立董事对关联交易、董事高管聘任、财务审计等事项有特别同意权。
us_claim: Under NYSE/Nasdaq listing rules and SEC regulations, listed companies must have a majority of independent directors on the board. The SEC and exchanges define independence based on lack of material relationships with the company (e.g., no compensation beyond director fees, no family/business ties). See NYSE Listed Company Manual Section 303A.02; Nasdaq Rule 5605(b)(1).
hk_claim: Under HKEX Listing Rules (Main Board Rule 3.10-3.13), listed companies must have at least three independent non-executive directors (INEDs), representing at least one-third of the board. INEDs must be independent of management and free from any interests that could interfere with independent judgment. The board must establish audit, remuneration, and nomination committees with INED majority. See HKEX Listing Rules 3.10A, 3.21.
notes: 三法域部分映射：独立董事制度在三法域均为上市公司强制要求，但比例和职能不同。中国法要求至少1/3且赋予特别同意权（veto on connected transactions），美国NYSE/Nasdaq要求过半数且强调committee独立性，香港法要求至少3名/1/3且审计委员会必须INED占多数。差异反映了不同监管模式对董事会独立性的量化与职能配置。

fact_summary: Blockchain-based corporate registration and tokenized shares are emerging in some US states (Wyoming), permitted in China only for non-securities digital assets, and not yet recognized under HK company law. / 区块链公司注册和代币化股份在美国部分州（怀俄明）兴起，在中国仅允许非证券数字资产，在香港公司法下尚未被承认。
cn_claim: 中国《公司法》和《证券法》不承认区块链代币作为公司股份的法定形式。中国人民银行等部委禁止代币发行融资（ICO）和虚拟货币交易。数字资产仅限于央行数字货币（CBDC）和经批准的区块链应用（如供应链金融），不得用于股权登记或交易。参见《关于防范代币发行融资风险的公告》（2017）。
us_claim: Under Wyoming law (W.S. 17-16-101 et seq.), a corporation may issue shares in tokenized form on a blockchain, and blockchain records may be maintained as the official stock ledger. Wyoming recognizes the validity of blockchain-based corporate records. Other states have followed with similar legislation. See Wyoming House Bill 185 (2021).
hk_claim: Under current Hong Kong law (Companies Ordinance Cap 622), there is no statutory recognition of blockchain-based share registers or tokenized equity. The Companies Registry maintains traditional paper/electronic registers. However, the SFC has issued guidelines on security token offerings (STOs) under Cap 571, treating tokenized securities as regulated products. See SFC Circular on STOs, November 2019.
notes: 三法域部分映射：区块链公司注册/代币化股权在美国部分州（Wyoming）已获法定承认，在中国被禁止用于股权（限于央行数字货币），香港对证券代币持监管态度（SFC STO指引）但公司法未承认。差异反映了金融科技创新与证券监管之间的不同平衡点：美国州法实验主义、中国金融审慎监管、香港产品监管路径。

fact_summary: An employee is terminated without cause in the US under at-will employment doctrine, while China requires statutory grounds for dismissal under Labor Contract Law, and HK requires reasonable notice or payment in lieu under Cap 57. / 美国员工在无固定期限雇佣原则下被无因解雇，中国《劳动合同法》要求法定解雇事由，香港依据《雇佣条例》Cap 57要求合理通知或代通知金。
cn_claim: 依据《劳动合同法》第39条、第40条、第41条，用人单位解除劳动合同必须有法定事由，包括严重违反规章制度、不能胜任工作、客观情况发生重大变化等。无正当理由解雇构成违法解除，劳动者可以要求继续履行合同或获得赔偿金（2N）。
us_claim: Under the employment-at-will doctrine recognized in most US states, an employer may terminate an employee for any reason (or no reason) at any time, without prior notice, except for reasons prohibited by statute (e.g., discrimination under Title VII, ADA, ADEA). See Payne v. Western & Atlantic Railroad, 81 Tenn. 507 (1884). Montana is the only state with a statutory just-cause requirement (Wrongful Discharge from Employment Act, MCA §39-2-901).
hk_claim: Under Employment Ordinance (Cap 57), an employer must give the employee notice of termination or payment in lieu of notice. Summary dismissal (without notice) is permitted only for serious misconduct (wilful disobedience, fraud, habitual neglect). For continuous contracts of 2+ years, an employee may claim unreasonable dismissal if the dismissal is not for a valid reason. See Cap 57 s.6-7; Cap 57 s.32A-32K.
notes: 三法域直接冲突：美国雇佣自由原则（at-will employment）允许无因即时解雇，中国法要求法定解除事由并施加违法解除赔偿（2N），香港法要求合理通知且长期员工可挑战不合理解雇。这是劳动法领域最显著的制度冲突，反映了美国市场自由主义、中国劳动者保护主义和英国普通法程序正义的不同价值排序。

fact_summary: Non-compete agreements are broadly enforceable in the US (with state variations), heavily restricted in China to senior personnel and trade secrets, and generally void under HK contract law as restraints of trade unless reasonable. / 竞业禁止协议在美国广泛可执行（州际有差异），在中国严格限制于高级管理人员和商业秘密，在香港依合同法一般作为限制贸易无效除非合理。
cn_claim: 依据《劳动合同法》第23条、第24条，竞业限制适用于用人单位的高级管理人员、高级技术人员和其他负有保密义务的人员。竞业限制期限不得超过二年，用人单位须按月支付经济补偿。2024年《最高人民法院关于审理劳动争议案件适用法律问题的解释（二）》进一步限制竞业禁止适用范围，禁止滥用。
us_claim: Under US law, non-compete agreements are generally enforceable if reasonable in scope, duration, and geographic area. However, states vary widely: California (Business & Professions Code §16600) generally voids non-competes, while Texas and other states enforce them. The FTC issued a rule banning most non-competes nationwide (2024), but the rule was vacated by the Fifth Circuit in Ryan LLC v. FTC, 2025 WL 1246799 (5th Cir. 2025). PENDING_VERIFICATION on federal appeal status.
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong common law, non-compete clauses are prima facie void as restraints of trade (Nordenfelt v. Maxim Nordenfelt Guns & Ammunition Co [1894] AC 535). They are enforceable only if the employer can demonstrate a legitimate proprietary interest (trade secrets, confidential information, customer connections) and the restriction is reasonable in scope, duration, and geographic area. See Airtours International Ltd v. British Airways plc [1997] HKCA 310.
notes: 三法域直接冲突：美国法（除加州外）广泛执行竞业禁止，中国法严格限制于高管/技术人员+2年期限+经济补偿，香港普通法以'限制贸易无效'为推定（Nordenfelt标准）。2024年FTC禁止令被第五巡回法院推翻（Ryan LLC v. FTC），使美国制度回归州法碎片化。差异反映了劳动力流动性、商业秘密保护与雇主契约自由之间的不同平衡。

fact_summary: Collective bargaining and union representation are protected under NLRA in the US but restricted to one official union in China under ACFTU monopoly, while HK recognizes trade unions but lacks a statutory collective bargaining framework. / 美国《国家劳动关系法》保护集体谈判和工会代表，中国只允许中华全国总工会的垄断性工会，香港承认工会但缺乏制定法集体谈判框架。
cn_claim: 依据《工会法》第2条、第6条，中国工会是中国共产党领导的职工自愿结合的工人阶级群众组织，中华全国总工会是各地方总工会和各产业工会全国组织的领导机关。中国实行单一工会制度，企业不得拒绝职工依法组建和参加工会，但不存在美国式的多工会竞争或独立集体谈判。集体合同由工会代表职工与企业签订。
us_claim: Under the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) 29 U.S.C. §§151-169, employees have the right to self-organization, to form, join, or assist labor organizations, to bargain collectively through representatives of their own choosing, and to engage in concerted activities. Employers are prohibited from interfering with these rights under §158(a). See NLRB v. Jones & Laughlin Steel Corp., 301 U.S. 1 (1937).
hk_claim: Under Employment Ordinance (Cap 57) and Trade Unions Ordinance (Cap 332), Hong Kong recognizes the right to form and join trade unions. However, there is no statutory duty on employers to engage in collective bargaining. Collective agreements are not legally enforceable as contracts unless specifically incorporated into individual employment contracts. See Kowloon Stock Exchange Staff Association v. Kowloon Stock Exchange [1984] HKLR 478.
notes: 三法域直接冲突：美国法（NLRA）保护多工会竞争和集体谈判权，中国法实行单一工会垄断（ACFTU）和自上而下的集体合同，香港法承认工会自由但缺乏集体谈判的强制机制。差异反映了美国法对劳动力市场 pluralism 的保护、中国法对工会统一领导的政治要求以及香港普通法对契约自由的偏好。

fact_summary: Overtime pay is calculated as 1.5x for hours over 40/week in the US under FLSA, while China requires 1.5x/2x/3x for extended hours/weekends/holidays, and HK requires rest days but does not mandate premium overtime pay unless specified in contract. / 美国《公平劳动标准法》要求超过40小时/周按1.5倍支付，中国要求延时/休息日/法定节假日分别按1.5/2/3倍支付，香港要求休息日但除非合同规定否则不强制加班费。
cn_claim: 依据《劳动法》第44条，安排劳动者延长工作时间的，支付不低于工资的百分之一百五十的工资报酬；休息日安排劳动者工作又不能安排补休的，支付不低于工资的百分之二百的工资报酬；法定休假日安排劳动者工作的，支付不低于工资的百分之三百的工资报酬。
us_claim: Under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) 29 U.S.C. §207, employers must pay covered employees overtime pay at a rate not less than one and one-half times the regular rate of pay for hours worked in excess of 40 per workweek. Certain exemptions apply for executive, administrative, and professional employees (white-collar exemptions). See 29 U.S.C. §213(a)(1).
hk_claim: Under Employment Ordinance (Cap 57), there is no statutory requirement for employers to pay overtime premium for work beyond normal hours, unless the employment contract or collective agreement provides for it. However, employees are entitled to rest days (not less than 1 day per 7 days) and statutory holidays. See Cap 57 s.17-18.
notes: 三法域直接冲突：美国法（FLSA §207）以40小时/周为门槛统一适用1.5倍，中国法（Labor Law Art. 44）按延时/休息日/节假日分三档（1.5/2/3倍），香港法无强制加班费（依赖合同约定）。差异反映了美国法对工作时间总量控制、中国法对节假日特殊保护的意识形态以及香港对契约自由的传统尊重。

fact_summary: Employment discrimination based on race, sex, and disability is prohibited under Title VII/ADA in the US, under Employment Law in HK for race and disability, but China lacks a comprehensive anti-discrimination statute and relies on general Labor Law and Constitution provisions. / 美国《民权法》第七章/ADA禁止基于种族、性别和残疾的就业歧视，香港《雇佣条例》和《残疾歧视条例》禁止种族和残疾歧视，但中国缺乏全面的反歧视制定法，仅依赖《劳动法》和《宪法》一般条款。
cn_claim: 中国尚无专门的《反就业歧视法》。《劳动法》第12条、第13条规定劳动者就业不因民族、种族、性别、宗教信仰不同而受歧视，但缺乏具体执法机构和民事救济程序。残疾保护主要依据《残疾人保障法》。实践中，反歧视诉讼往往通过一般人格权或劳动争议程序处理，缺乏明确的歧视举证规则。
us_claim: Under Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (42 U.S.C. §2000e-2), it is unlawful for an employer to discriminate against any individual because of race, color, religion, sex, or national origin. The Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) 42 U.S.C. §12112 prohibits discrimination against qualified individuals with disabilities. The burden-shifting framework is established in McDonnell Douglas Corp. v. Green, 411 U.S. 792 (1973).
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong law, the Race Discrimination Ordinance (Cap 602) and Disability Discrimination Ordinance (Cap 487) prohibit employment discrimination on grounds of race and disability. Sex discrimination is prohibited under the Sex Discrimination Ordinance (Cap 480). The Equal Opportunities Commission (EOC) enforces these laws, and complainants may seek remedies in the District Court. See Cap 602 s.6-8; Cap 487 s.11-12.
notes: 三法域直接冲突：美国拥有最完善的反歧视制定法体系（Title VII/ADA）和判例法举证框架（McDonnell Douglas），香港有专门反歧视条例（Cap 480/487/602）和EOC执法，中国缺乏专门立法和执法机构，依赖零散条款和一般人格权保护。差异反映了美国民权运动驱动的法律深化、英国反歧视传统的殖民地移植以及中国社会保障法体系的滞后性。

fact_summary: Severance pay is generally not required in US at-will employment (except WARN Act for mass layoffs), while China requires statutory economic compensation (N) for ordinary dismissal, and HK requires severance payment for redundancy under Cap 57. / 美国雇佣自由制下一般不要求遣散费（大规模裁员除外），中国普通解雇要求法定经济补偿（N），香港裁员要求依据《雇佣条例》Cap 57支付遣散费。
cn_claim: 依据《劳动合同法》第46条、第47条，用人单位依照本法第40条（无过失性辞退）、第41条（经济性裁员）解除劳动合同的，应当向劳动者支付经济补偿。经济补偿按劳动者在本单位工作的年限，每满一年支付一个月工资的标准向劳动者支付（N）。违法解除的支付2N。
us_claim: Under US federal law, there is no general requirement for severance pay for individual terminations. However, the Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act (WARN Act, 29 U.S.C. §2101-2109) requires 60 days' advance notice for mass layoffs or plant closings affecting 50+ employees. Severance is typically governed by individual employment contracts or company policies. See 29 U.S.C. §2102.
hk_claim: Under Employment Ordinance (Cap 57) sections 31A-31Z, an employee employed under a continuous contract for not less than 24 months is eligible for severance payment if dismissed by reason of redundancy. The amount is calculated as two-thirds of the last full month's wages (capped at HK$22,500) multiplied by the number of years of service. See Cap 57 s.31B.
notes: 三法域直接冲突：美国法无个别解雇遣散费要求（仅WARN Act对大规模裁员），中国法要求按工龄支付N（或违法解除2N），香港法要求24个月工龄+裁员原因+2/3月薪（封顶）的遣散费。差异反映了美国雇佣自由制下的契约自由、中国法对劳动者工龄的累积保护以及香港英国传统对裁员补偿的渐进式保障。

fact_summary: Tipped employees in the US may be paid a sub-minimum cash wage (federal tipped minimum $2.13/hour) under FLSA, while no such sub-minimum wage exists in China or Hong Kong for tipped/service workers. / 美国《公平劳动标准法》允许给小费员工支付低于最低现金工资（联邦小费最低工资2.13美元/小时），中国和香港不存在此类低于最低工资的给小费/服务业工人工资。
cn_claim: 中国《劳动法》第48条和《最低工资规定》规定，用人单位支付劳动者的工资不得低于当地最低工资标准。中国不存在给小费员工的次最低工资（sub-minimum wage）制度。所有劳动者（包括餐饮业服务人员）均受最低工资标准保护，小费收入不得计入最低工资。
us_claim: Under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) 29 U.S.C. §203(m), an employer may pay a tipped employee a cash wage of not less than $2.13 per hour (federal tipped minimum), provided that the employee's tips plus the cash wage equal at least the federal minimum wage ($7.25/hour). If tips are insufficient, the employer must make up the difference. See 29 U.S.C. §203(m); 29 CFR §531.59.
hk_claim: Under the Minimum Wage Ordinance (Cap 608), there is no distinction between tipped and non-tipped employees. All employees must be paid at least the statutory minimum wage (currently HK$40 per hour). Tips, gratuities, and service charges are not counted towards the minimum wage unless they are contractual wages. See Cap 608 s.8.
notes: 三法域不对称：美国法存在独特的小费次最低工资制度（FLSA §203(m)），中国和香港均无此制度。这是美国服务业特有的制度遗产，源于小费文化（tipping culture）的立法承认。中国法对最低工资采取刚性统一保护，香港法同样要求最低工资适用于所有雇员。差异反映了美国劳动力市场的分层支付传统与东亚统一最低工资理念的冲突。

fact_summary: Religious accommodation in employment is required under US Title VII (undue hardship standard), while no equivalent statutory religious accommodation duty exists in China or Hong Kong. / 美国《民权法》第七章要求雇主提供宗教便利（undue hardship标准），中国和香港不存在对等的制定法宗教便利义务。
cn_claim: 中国《劳动法》和《就业促进法》未明确规定雇主的宗教便利义务。中国实行宗教与就业分离原则，雇主通常无义务因员工宗教信仰调整工作安排。宗教自由受《宪法》第36条保护，但在就业领域缺乏具体的反歧视和便利立法。
us_claim: Under Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (42 U.S.C. §2000e(j)), an employer must reasonably accommodate an employee's religious practices or beliefs unless doing so would cause an undue hardship on the employer's business. The undue hardship standard was modified in Groff v. DeJoy, 600 U.S. 447 (2023), requiring the employer to show the burden is substantial in the overall context of the business. See Trans World Airlines, Inc. v. Hardison, 432 U.S. 63 (1977) (overruled in part by Groff).
hk_claim: Hong Kong law does not impose a specific statutory duty on employers to accommodate religious practices in the workplace. The Basic Law (Art. 32) and the Bill of Rights Ordinance (Cap 383) protect religious freedom, but employment discrimination laws (Cap 480, 487, 602) do not explicitly cover religious discrimination or accommodation requirements. See Chan Wah v. Hang Hau Rural Committee [2000] 1 HKLRD 113.
notes: 三法域不对称：美国法（Title VII §2000e(j)）要求宗教便利（经Groff v. DeJoy修正为'实质性负担'标准），中国和香港无此义务。这反映了美国宗教自由在就业法中的特殊地位（受第一修正案和民权法双重强化），而中国法奉行世俗主义、香港法遵循英国传统不将宗教歧视纳入核心就业歧视法。差异根植于宗教-国家关系的不同宪政安排。

fact_summary: Mandatory individual arbitration agreements in employment are generally enforceable in the US (FAA) but restricted in China and Hong Kong where labor disputes must go through labor arbitration/tribunals. / 美国《联邦仲裁法》下雇佣中的强制个人仲裁协议一般可执行，但中国和香港限制该做法，劳动争议须通过劳动仲裁/审裁处处理。
cn_claim: 依据《劳动争议调解仲裁法》第5条，发生劳动争议，当事人不愿协商、调解不成或达成调解协议后不履行的，可以向劳动争议仲裁委员会申请仲裁；对仲裁裁决不服的，可以向人民法院提起诉讼。劳动争议仲裁是诉讼前置程序，且用人单位与劳动者不得通过协议排除法定仲裁管辖。
us_claim: Under the Federal Arbitration Act (FAA, 9 U.S.C. §§1-16) and Supreme Court precedent, mandatory individual arbitration agreements in employment are generally enforceable, and class action waivers are permitted. See AT&T Mobility LLC v. Concepcion, 563 U.S. 333 (2011); Epic Systems Corp. v. Lewis, 137 S. Ct. 2093 (2017). The FAA preempts state laws that invalidate arbitration agreements. See Volt Info. Sciences v. Board of Trustees, 489 U.S. 468 (1989).
hk_claim: Under Hong Kong law, the Labour Tribunal has exclusive jurisdiction over employment disputes involving monetary claims or breaches of employment contract (up to HK$800,000). Parties cannot contractually waive the right to bring claims before the Labour Tribunal. See Labour Tribunal Ordinance (Cap 25) s.6-7; Reformation Labour Tribunal jurisdiction rules.
notes: 三法域不对称：美国法（FAA + Epic Systems）允许强制个人仲裁和集体诉讼豁免，中国法（Labor Dispute Mediation and Arbitration Law Art. 5）要求劳动仲裁前置且不可协议排除，香港法（Labour Tribunal Ordinance Cap 25）赋予审裁处专属管辖权。差异反映了美国法对私人争议解决（ADR）的推崇、中国法对劳动者程序保护的法定主义以及香港法对劳动法庭专属管辖的行政安排。

fact_summary: All three jurisdictions establish a minimum wage floor that employers must pay to covered employees. / 三法域均建立了雇主必须支付给适用员工的最低工资底线。
cn_claim: 依据《劳动法》第48条和《最低工资规定》，国家实行最低工资保障制度。用人单位支付劳动者的工资不得低于当地最低工资标准。最低工资标准由省、自治区、直辖市人民政府规定，报国务院备案。
us_claim: Under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) 29 U.S.C. §206, employers must pay covered employees a minimum wage of not less than $7.25 per hour (federal minimum). States may establish higher minimum wages. The minimum wage applies to all non-exempt employees. See 29 U.S.C. §206(a)(1).
hk_claim: Under the Minimum Wage Ordinance (Cap 608), all employees (except certain exempt categories such as student interns and live-in domestic workers) must be paid at least the statutory minimum wage, currently set at HK$40 per hour (effective May 2023). The Minimum Wage Commission reviews the rate biennially. See Cap 608 s.6.
notes: 三法域映射：最低工资制度（minimum wage）在三法域均为劳动法的基础性保护。中国法（Labor Law Art. 48 + Minimum Wage Regulation）实行地区差异化标准，美国法（FLSA §206）实行联邦底线+州法叠加，香港法（Cap 608）实行统一法定标准（HK$40/小时）。三者均试图通过工资底线防止劳动力市场剥削，是劳动法全球化的核心共识。

fact_summary: Working hours are regulated in all three jurisdictions with standard limits and rest period requirements. / 三法域均对工作时间进行规制，设定标准上限和休息期要求。
cn_claim: 依据《劳动法》第36条、第38条，国家实行劳动者每日工作时间不超过八小时、平均每周工作时间不超过四十四小时的工时制度。用人单位应当保证劳动者每周至少休息一日。特殊工时（综合计算工时、不定时工时）须经劳动行政部门批准。
us_claim: Under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) 29 U.S.C. §207, covered employees must receive overtime pay for hours worked over 40 in a workweek. The FLSA does not limit the number of hours per day or per week for adult employees, but child labor restrictions apply. The standard workweek is generally considered 40 hours for overtime purposes. See 29 U.S.C. §207(a)(1).
hk_claim: Under Employment Ordinance (Cap 57), there is no statutory limit on the number of working hours per day or week for adult employees. However, employees are entitled to rest days (not less than 1 day in every period of 7 days) and statutory holidays (12 days per year). See Cap 57 s.17-18. For certain industries (e.g., young persons in industrial undertakings), hours are restricted under the Employment of Young Persons and Children Ordinance (Cap 57B).
notes: 三法域映射：工作时间规制在三法域均存在，但形式不同。中国法设定每日8小时/每周44小时上限（Labor Law Art. 36），美国法以40小时/周为加班门槛（FLSA §207），香港法无每日/每周上限但保障休息日（Cap 57 s.17）。三者的共同目标都是防止过度劳动，但干预强度不同：中国直接限制工时总量，美国以经济激励（加班费）间接调节，香港以休息权保障为核心。

fact_summary: Workplace safety and health obligations are imposed on employers in all three jurisdictions. / 三法域均对雇主施加工作场所安全与健康义务。
cn_claim: 依据《安全生产法》第4条、第44条，生产经营单位必须遵守本法和其他有关安全生产的法律、法规，加强安全生产管理，建立健全全员安全生产责任制和安全生产规章制度。用人单位必须为劳动者提供符合国家规定的劳动安全卫生条件和必要的劳动防护用品。
us_claim: Under the Occupational Safety and Health Act (OSHA) 29 U.S.C. §651-678, employers must provide a workplace free from recognized hazards that are causing or are likely to cause death or serious physical harm. Employers must comply with OSHA standards, maintain records of workplace injuries, and provide personal protective equipment. See 29 U.S.C. §654(a)(1) (general duty clause).
hk_claim: Under the Occupational Safety and Health Ordinance (Cap 509), employers must ensure the safety and health at work of all employees by providing a safe workplace, safe systems of work, and necessary information, instruction, training, and supervision. The ordinance applies to all workplaces except domestic premises. See Cap 509 s.6(1).
notes: 三法域映射：职业安全与健康义务（workplace safety）是三法域劳动法的共同基石。中国法（Work Safety Law Art. 4, 44）、美国法（OSHA §654 general duty clause）和香港法（Cap 509 s.6）均要求雇主提供安全工作环境、必要防护和培训。三者均体现了国际劳工组织（ILO）公约的国内法转化，是劳动法全球化中最成熟的制度趋同领域。

fact_summary: Workers injured in the course of employment are entitled to compensation in all three jurisdictions through statutory insurance or employer liability schemes. / 三法域中雇员因工受伤均有权通过法定保险或雇主责任计划获得赔偿。
cn_claim: 依据《工伤保险条例》第2条、第14条，中华人民共和国境内的企业、事业单位、社会团体、民办非企业单位等组织的职工应当参加工伤保险，由用人单位缴纳工伤保险费。职工在工作时间和工作场所内，因工作原因受到事故伤害的，应当认定为工伤，享受工伤保险待遇。
us_claim: Under state workers' compensation laws (no federal general workers' compensation statute), employees injured in the course of employment are entitled to medical benefits, wage replacement, and disability benefits.
